content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

February 5, 1990


Kirk Alan Pessner
Political Reports Unit
Nielsen, Merksamer, Hodgson,
Parrinello & Mueller
650 California Street, Suite 2650
San Francisco, CA 94108

Re: Your Request For Advice
Our File No. A-90-045

Dear Mr. Pessner:

You have requested written confirmation of telephone advice
provided to you under the campaign disclosure provisions of the
Political Reform Act.1/ You requested this advice on behalf of
your client, Gary J. Flynn, a state measure proponent.

Your letter dated January 10, 1990, accurately summarizes my
advice concerning the requirement to file campaign disclosure
statements under Section 84200.5(f) in connection with a proposed
initiative for which petitions were never circulated. The
petitions were not circulated because the proposed initiative was
amended and provided with a new title and summary and, therefore,
new deadline dates were established for the qualification process.

If you have additional questions, please call me at (916)
322-5662.

Sincerely,

Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel

[signature]
By: Jeanne Pritchard
Division Chief
Technical Assistance and
Analysis Division


1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

sections.question_synthetic: Whether campaign disclosure statements must be filed under Section 84200.5(f) for a proposed initiative when petitions were never circulated due to the initiative being amended with a new title and summary establishing new qualification deadline dates.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The letter confirms that the earlier telephone advice provided was accurate regarding the campaign disclosure statement filing requirements under Section 84200.5(f) for an initiative where petitions were never circulated because the proposed initiative was amended with a new title and summary, establishing new deadline dates for the qualification process.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: ocr; LLM: Document is a brief confirmation letter of prior telephone advice rather than a detailed opinion. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections are present; substantive legal analysis appears embedded in body paragraphs. Confidence reduced slightly due to OCR quality issues and lack of traditional Q&A structure.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether campaign disclosure statements must be filed under Section 84200.5(f) for a proposed initiative when petitions were never circulated due to the initiative being amended with a new title and summary establishing new qualification deadline dates.

CONCLUSION: The letter confirms that the earlier telephone advice provided was accurate regarding the campaign disclosure statement filing requirements under Section 84200.5(f) for an initiative where petitions were never circulated because the proposed initiative was amended with a new title and summary, establishing new deadline dates for the qualification process.

Your letter dated January 10, 1990, accurately summarizes my advice concerning the requirement to file campaign disclosure statements under Section 84200.5(f) in connection with a proposed initiative for which petitions were never circulated. Donovan confirms written advice to Kirk Pessner regarding campaign disclosure filing requirements under Section 84200.5(f) for a state measure initiative that was amended before petitions were circulated, resulting in new qualification deadline dates.
